Kantō-kai.description: The Kantō-kai was a Japanese underworld organization formed by Yoshio Kodama in 1964, named for the Kantō region. It was envisioned as a secret national police force to promote far-right views. The group disbanded in January 1965 after only fifteen months, but was a crucial step in uniting post-war gangs into the modern yakuza.

descriptions: Inagawa-kai's founder headed the Kanto-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1960s, he headed the short-lived Kanto-kai, a federation of Kantō region gangs organized by Yoshio Kodama.

descriptions: Matsuba-kai was a member group of the Kanto-kai coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was formed of seven yakuza groups (including the Sumiyoshi-kai and Matsuba-kai)

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai was a member group of the Kanto-kai coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was formed of seven yakuza groups (including the Sumiyoshi-kai and Matsuba-kai)

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi refused to join Kodama's envisioned organization, leading to Kantō-kai being limited to the Kantō region.
edges.evidence_quote: Kodama had originally envisioned a Japan-wide gangster society, but in 1963 Kazuo Taoka and his Kansai -based Yamaguchi-gumi gang refused to join, leaving Kodama with a Kantō-heavy organization.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi refused to join Kanto-kai, indicating non-cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: he notably refused to join the Kanto-kai, an inter-yakuza confederation in 1963.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi withdrew from talks to form the Kanto-kai, so it was not part of the coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: Kazuo Taoka withdrew the Yamaguchi-gumi early on in talks, leaving Kodama with a Tokyo-centered group that would become known as the Kanto-kai

descriptions: Carpino killed presumed affiliates of Tratraculo in 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: vengono uccisi i fratelli Ettore e Angelo Talarico presunti affiliati alla cosca "Tratraculo"

Orekhovskaya gang.description: A powerful Russian criminal organization based in Moscow, active from the late 1980s to early 2000s. Founded in 1988 by Sergey Timofeev, it consisted mainly of young former athletes. The gang was involved in extortion, racketeering, money laundering, and murder, and is believed responsible for at least 6,800 murders. It split into warring factions in 1994 and was eventually absorbed by the Solntsevskaya Bratva.

descriptions: The Chechen mafia was in conflict with the Orekhovskaya gang.
edges.evidence_quote: This brought them into conflict with the Slavic gangs, including the Solntsevskaya bratva and the Orekhovskaya gang

descriptions: Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya allied against Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 the gang merged with the Solntsevskaya bratva to combat the growing threat of Chechen mafia gangs.

descriptions: Izmailovskaya gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kuntsevskaya gang, Caucasian gangs, Izmailovskaya gang

descriptions: Kuntsevskaya gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kuntsevskaya gang, Caucasian gangs, Izmailovskaya gang

descriptions: The alliance between Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya bratva ended by 1997.
edges.evidence_quote: However, by 1997 the alliance had split and Todorov was again running an independent gang.

descriptions: Orekhovskaya merged with Solntsevskaya bratva in 1989 to fight Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 the gang merged with the Solntsevskaya bratva to combat the growing threat of Chechen mafia gangs.

descriptions: Remnants of Orekhovskaya were absorbed by Solntsevskaya Bratva after the split.
edges.evidence_quote: what was left of the gang was swallowed up by the Solntsevskaya Bratva

descriptions: Ivankov had a conflict with the Orekhovskaya gang leader, resulting in the leader's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Ivankov got into another altercation with drug kingpin and head of the Orekhovskaya gang, Segei "Sylvester" Timofeyev, ending with the latter murdered a month later.

descriptions: Orekhovskaya OPG is a specific group within the Russian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Any of the mafia's groups may be referred to as an "Organized Criminal Group" (OPG, from Russian "организованная преступная группа"). This is sometimes modified to include a specific name, such as the Orekhovskaya OPG.

descriptions: Solntsevskaya allied with Orekhovskaya gang against Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Together with the Orekhovskaya gang and other Slavic mobs, the Solntsevo made an alliance to drive the Chechens out.

descriptions: Mascot Street Killas are listed as allies of Lucerne Street Doggz.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mascot Street Killas

descriptions: Navi Clan modeled itself on the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Navi Clan ("Holy Church of the White Race") was founded in Russia in 1996 and adopted Klan-style robes and rituals modeled on those of the Ku Klux Klan.

descriptions: Asian Boys Insanity formed Monterey Park Asian Boyz after merging with Long Beach Asian Boyz.

descriptions: Asian Boys Insanity merged with Monterey Park Asian Boyz to form West Side Asian Boyz.
edges.evidence_quote: Then later in the mid-1990s, both Asian Boys Insanity and Monterey Park Asian Boyz (MP ABZ) became what is now known as West Side Asian Boyz (WS ABZ).

descriptions: Asian Boys Insanity merged with Long Beach Asian Boyz.
edges.evidence_quote: One of the Asian gang that integrated with Long Beach Asian Boyz during the early 1990s was the Asian Boys Insanity (ABI).

'Ndrina Cicero.description: The 'Ndrina Cicero is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Calabrian mafia organization, based in Cosenza, Italy. It is involved in criminal activities such as drug trafficking and murder. Members include Domenico, Osvaldo, and Francesco Cicero. The clan has been targeted by law enforcement, with assets seized and affiliates arrested.

descriptions: 'Ndrina Cicero is a subunit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Cicero è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Cosenza.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños , Aryan Brotherhood , Peckerwoods

Al-Qaeda.description: Al-Qaeda is a pan-Islamist militant organization founded in 1988 by Osama bin Laden and other veterans of the Soviet-Afghan War. It aims to establish a global Islamic caliphate through jihad, and has conducted major attacks including the 1998 U.S. embassy bombings and the September 11 attacks. The group operates primarily in the Middle East, South Asia, and Africa, and has been designated a terrorist organization by the UN and many countries. Despite the death of bin Laden in 2011, it remains active under new leadership.

descriptions: The text compares 'Ndrangheta's structure to that of al-Qaida, but does not assert any direct relationship between the two organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: La relazione della commissione parlamentare antimafia del 20 febbraio 2008 afferma che la 'ndrangheta «ha una struttura tentacolare priva di direzione strategica ma caratterizzata da una sorta di intelligenza organica», e la paragona alla struttura del movimento terroristico islamico al-Qaida.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda provided initial funding to Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: The government noted that initial funding for ASG came from al-Qaeda through the brother-in-law of Osama bin Laden, Mohammed Jamal Khalifa.

edges.evidence_quote: 3 February 2014 : Beginning of Al-Qaeda–Islamic State conflict

edges.evidence_quote: Non-state opponents: Al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Qaeda cut ties with Islamic State and denounced it, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, Ayman al-Zawahiri, the leader of Al-Qaeda, announced that his group Al-Qaeda had cut ties with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant and denounced ISIL after being unable to reconcile a conflict between them and the al-Qaeda affiliate al-Nusra Front.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda disavowed relations with ISIL in February 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, after continued tensions, al-Qaeda publicly disavowed any relations with ISIL.

descriptions: Islamic State follows the ideology of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: IS adheres to global jihadist principles and follows the hard-line ideology of al-Qaeda and many other modern-day jihadist groups.

descriptions: The Islamic State's predecessor fought alongside al-Qaeda in Iraq during the insurgency.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.

descriptions: Islamic State originated from al-Qaeda in Iraq, which was an affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The group later changed its name to Islamic State of Iraq and Levant for about a year, before declaring itself to be a worldwide caliphate, called simply the Islamic State.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Al-Qaeda AQIS AGH

descriptions: Al-Qaeda founder Osama bin Laden funded LeT's founding.
edges.evidence_quote: It was founded in 1985–1986 by Hafiz Saeed, Zafar Iqbal Shehbaz, Abdullah Azzam and several other Islamist mujahideen with funding from Osama bin Laden during the Soviet–Afghan War.

descriptions: LeT provides support to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: While the primary focus for the Lashkar is the operations in Indian Kashmir, it has frequently provided support to other international terrorist groups. Primary among these is the al-Qaeda Network in Afghanistan.

descriptions: LeT and al-Qaeda-linked 313 Brigade are mentioned together, but no direct relationship stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Supporters of Pakistan-based militant groups, including Lashkar-e-Taiba and the al-Qaeda-linked 313 Brigade, posted videos on TikTok, YouTube, and Google from here.

descriptions: MAB publicly condemned Al-Qaeda's terrorist activities.
edges.evidence_quote: MAB has condemned Al-Qaeda terrorist activity in the UK.

descriptions: Osama bin Laden of al-Qaeda criticized the Muslim Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: Osama bin Laden criticized the Brotherhood, and accused it of betraying jihad and the ideals of Qutb.

descriptions: The Muslim Brotherhood opposed al-Qaeda and ISIS.
edges.evidence_quote: The report noted that the Brotherhood had 'rejected violence as a matter of official policy and opposed al-Qa'ida and ISIS'

descriptions: Some academic approaches draw a connection between the Muslim Brotherhood and al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The first approach draws a clear connection between the Muslim Brotherhood and terrorist organizations such as al‑Qaeda.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda affiliates are enemies of SDF.
edges.evidence_quote: Major enemies include al-Qaeda affiliates, the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL), the Syrian National Army (TFSA), the Turkish Armed Forces, and their allies.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda is described as an ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: before it was overthrown by an American-led invasion after the September 11 attacks carried out by the Taliban's ally al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Qaeda is a non-state ally of the Taliban, though currently denied.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state allies ... Al-Qaeda (currently denied)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda supported the Taliban during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: During the war, the Taliban were supported by several militant outfits which include the Haqqani network, Al-Qaeda and the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda is an ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: the September 11 attacks carried out by the Taliban's ally al-Qaeda

descriptions: Brothers for Life absorbed the remnants of the Muslim Brotherhood Movement after it was dismantled in 2011.
edges.evidence_quote: In turn, B4L took over in the area, absorbing the remnants of MBM, and prolific Assyrian street-gang DLASTHR (The Last Hour), including member Michael Odisho.

descriptions: Brothers for Life absorbed MBM, a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos had previously been aligned with Sydney's MBM gang, which had since been absorbed by B4L.

Swamp Angels.description: The Swamp Angels were a New York City waterfront street gang active from the 1850s to the 1890s. Based in Gotham Court on Cherry Street in Lower Manhattan, they used the sewers to raid East River dockyards and sell stolen cargo quickly. Their activities led to police sniper patrols and underground battles. The gang eventually merged with other waterfront gangs into the White Hand Gang.

descriptions: Swamp Angels merged with rival waterfront gangs to form the White Hand Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: until eventually they merged with the rival waterfront gangs into the White Hand Gang at the end of the 19th century.

descriptions: La Familia has connections with the Flores Camargo cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: It has been known that La Familia has certain connections with the Flores Camargo cartel, a small drug trafficking group that operates in Atlanta.

edges.target: Brigade of the Trustees of Raqqa
descriptions: Brigade of the Trustees of Raqqa was a local affiliate of Ahrar al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar al-Sham established a local affiliate known as Brigade of the Trustees of Raqqa

descriptions: Musolino and Zoccali were on opposite sides of a fight that led to a faida.
edges.evidence_quote: La storia del " Brigante Musolino " inizia il 28 ottobre 1897 quando scoppia una rissa rusticana nell'osteria della Frasca, a Santo Stefano in Aspromonte , per una partita di nocciole: da un lato Musolino e Antonio Filastò, dall'altro i fratelli Vincenzo e Stefano Zoccali, oltre ad un loro compagno.

descriptions: TMD and Hackney Boys were in a violent conflict with tit-for-tat killings.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, TMD had a conflict with another local gang known as the Hackney Boys (also known as Hackney Mandem, or HMD). The conflict resulted in a number of tit-for-tat killings.

descriptions: Infobox lists Hackney Boys as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hackney Boys

descriptions: Satanas is allied with Temple Street.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Avenue King Crips , Temple Street (gang)

Cortesi brothers.description: Four Italian-born siblings who operated as gangsters in London in the early 20th century, involved in protection racketeering of gamblers and bookmakers. They were rivals of the Sabini family, culminating in a 1922 shootout at the Fratellanza Social Club. Augustus and Enrico were jailed for attempted murder; the group disbanded after the brothers' deaths between 1938 and 1972.

descriptions: The Cortesi brothers were initially close to the Sabini family but later became rivals over profit sharing.
edges.evidence_quote: They were close to criminals in the English Italian Sabini family. However, the two families became rivals because the Cortesi brothers believed they were being denied a fair share of the proceeds from bookmaking and West End gambling clubs.

descriptions: Cortesi brothers challenged Sabinis but were fended off.
edges.evidence_quote: Sabini managed to fend off challenges from rival gangs such as the Cortesi brothers from Saffron Hill

descriptions: Dattilo is listed as an ally of Giampà in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dattilo, Iannazzo

descriptions: Iannazzo lists Dattilo as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Cannizzaro-Daponte, Pagliuso, Giampà, De Luca, Dattilo

descriptions: Los Monguis are aligned with Los Chapitos.
edges.evidence_quote: Two members of 'Los Monguis', a group aligned with Los Chapitos, were killed

descriptions: Ghazi Brigade worked closely with TTP in 2009, jointly planning attacks.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2009 the Ghazi Brigade worked closely with the TTP during military operations in the Swat Valley, and the two groups jointly planned attacks on western targets in Islamabad.

descriptions: The Fezza-De Vivo clan succeeded the Nuova Famiglia's criminal legacy.
edges.evidence_quote: L'eredità criminale di Olivieri è stata raccolta negli anni da uomini a lui vicini, intorno ai quali si è strutturato il clan Fezza-De Vivo

descriptions: Stasi provided military advisers to SWAPO.
edges.evidence_quote: Other Stasi agents worked as military advisers to African Marxist guerrillas ... They included the Namibian SWAPO

descriptions: The ADL opposed the Klan's campaign against private schools.
edges.evidence_quote: The Jewish Anti-Defamation League of B'nai B'rith, founded in the early 20th century in response to attacks on Jewish Americans —including the lynching of Leo Frank in Atlanta—opposed the Klan's campaign to prohibit private schools

descriptions: La Nueva Administración is a splinter group of Beltrán Leyva.
edges.evidence_quote: Fragmentación ... - La Nueva Administración en Morelos y Ciudad de México .

Hop Sing Tong.description: The Hop Sing Tong is a Chinese American Tong established in 1875. It has multiple branches across the United States, including in San Francisco, Los Angeles, Portland, Seattle, and Denver. Some branches are now defunct.

descriptions: Hop Sing Tong is listed as an ally of Black Dragons in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Wah Ching , Vietnamese Boyz Gang, Oriental Lazy Boyz , Asian Boyz , Hop Sing Tong, United Bamboo Triad, 14k Triad

descriptions: Allen Leung held leadership roles in both organizations simultaneously.
edges.evidence_quote: Allen Leung was president of the Hop Sing Tong (HST) and dragon head of the CKT

descriptions: Hop Sing Tong was in conflict with Bing Kong Tong during a Tong war.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hop Sing and Suey Sing Tongs allied against the Bing Kong Tong, instigating one of the most violent Tong wars

descriptions: Hop Sing and Suey Sing Tongs formed an alliance against Bing Kong Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hop Sing and Suey Sing Tongs allied against the Bing Kong Tong

descriptions: The infobox lists Hop Sing Tong as a rival of the Joe Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wah Ching , Hop Sing Tong

descriptions: Hop Sing Tong is listed as an ally of Wah Ching in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hop Sing Tong

descriptions: The Wah Ching were absorbed into the Hop Sing Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: By approximately 1968, the Wah Ching were absorbed into the Hop Sing Tong

descriptions: D-Company cooperated with ISI in providing logistics for the 2008 Mumbai attacks.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2008, D-Company provided logistics for the 2008 Mumbai attacks along with Inter-Services Intelligence.

descriptions: Alleged strategic alliance between D-Company and ISI.
edges.evidence_quote: a US Congressional report alleged that the D-Company was a '5,000-member criminal syndicate operating mostly in Pakistan, India, and the United Arab Emirates,' which has a 'strategic alliance' with ISI

descriptions: Many Rock Machine members came from the Merciless Riders.
edges.evidence_quote: The majority of the Rock Machine were former members of the Merciless Riders, the Executeurs and the SS.
